mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day equity returns with an interaction term coefficient insignificant at p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.4 percent are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55 percent of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields a negative or zero 20-day forward return in over 60 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit margins, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly industrial production declines exceeding 0.5 percent are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of observations.
